observations.text: In cross-evolution, gene == meme: a gene is a transmissible meme carried by skills, not a shallow file/script/documentation presence check.
Machine-readable registry and skill-local research artifacts should live in JSON; markdown should explain meanings and protocol.
Agent observation needs decomposed coverage/profiles instead of wide Gene x Skill matrices.
When local context already scopes ownership, use short artifact names instead of repeating the domain prefix.
Cross-evolution should proactively observe and nudge, but only perform narrow safe mutations unless semantic skill changes are explicitly confirmed.
When a repeated practice can travel across projects or skills, codify it as a portable standard rather than leaving it implicit.
Meta-comments and meta-protocols preserve the process behind the process: why rules exist, when they apply, and how they evolve.
Gene == meme is itself a gene-meme: it is a transmissible standard for deciding what counts as evolutionary material.
Any gene-meme can be decomposed, recombined, and composed to seek emergent properties and new gene-memes.
Soft proactivity has a bounded Horizontal Gene Transfer Mode: one donor, one gene-meme, one recipient, one expected behavior change, then stop.
